answer:
Yeah, so, I’d say one pretty memorable time I changed my opinion was about going to live concerts. For the longest time, I was honestly pretty skeptical about them. My original thought was, you know, “Why spend all that money and deal with crowds when you can just listen to music at home?” It seemed way more comfortable and, well, convenient.

But then, a couple years back, a friend of mine basically insisted that I go see this band we both liked, live in concert. At first, I kind of dragged my feet—figured it wouldn’t be worth it. But, wow, once I got there, it was like a whole different world. The energy of the crowd, everyone singing along, the lights and the atmosphere…it gave me chills! Plus, seeing the band perform in person made me appreciate their talent on another level. It was honestly electric.

So now, my outlook is totally different. I still love listening to music at home, no doubt, but catching a live show every now and then? That’s something I actually look forward to. It’s become one of my favorite ways to spend an evening, especially with good company. Live music just hits different, you know?
title:
Talk about a time that you changed your opinion about something. 
What was your original opinion? 
What happened that made you change your mind? 
What is your opinion now?
